skill.name: seedance-v2
skill.description: Generate cinematic short-form video with ByteDance Seedance 2.0 Pro on RunComfy. Documents Seedance 2.0 Pro's strengths (multi-modal references — up to 9 images, 3 videos, 3 audio — synchronized in-pass audio with natural lip-sync, cinematic motion refinement), the 4–15s duration schema, and when to route to HappyHorse 1.0 / Wan 2.7 / Kling instead. Calls `runcomfy run bytedance/seedance-v2/pro` through the local RunComfy CLI. Triggers on "seedance", "seedance 2", "seedance v2", "seedance pro", "bytedance video", or any explicit ask to generate video with this model.
skill.summary: Generate cinematic AI video with ByteDance Seedance 2.0 Pro, featuring native lip-sync and multi-modal reference support on RunComfy.
skill.author: doany-ai

security_audit.summary: All 89 static findings are false positives. The 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' flags are markdown code fences containing documentation examples, not executable code. 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' flags are false positives on 'seed' as a video generation parameter. 'System reconnaissance' flags are false positives on documentation text. Network and filesystem references are legitimate RunComfy API endpoints and token storage paths documented in the skill. This is a documentation-only skill for a video generation CLI with no malicious intent.

content.user_title: Generate Cinematic AI Video with Seedance 2.0 Pro
content.value_statement: Creating professional video content requires expensive equipment and expertise. Seedance 2.0 Pro on RunComfy enables AI-powered cinematic video generation with native lip-sync, multi-modal references, and production-ready output in seconds.
actual_capabilities: Generate 4-15 second cinematic videos with ByteDance Seedance 2.0 Pro model
Create lip-synced spokesperson and dialogue content with native in-pass audio
Use up to 9 image references, 3 video clips, and 3 audio references in a single generation
Guide camera motion and tone using plain language directives
Reproduce results using seed control for variant testing
limitations: Video duration limited to 4-15 seconds per generation
Maximum resolution is 720p on the playground variant
Reference media must be 2-15 seconds in length and audio under 15MB
Lip-sync quality depends on prompt clarity and may not be perfect in all conditions

use_cases.title: Marketing and Advertising Teams
use_cases.description: Create brand-consistent spokesperson ads and social media content with natural lip-sync. Use image references to maintain brand identity across multiple videos.

use_cases.title: Content Creators and Filmmakers
use_cases.description: Generate cinematic previsualization sequences for film projects. Combine multiple reference media types to establish visual continuity before production.

use_cases.title: Multilingual Brand Narratives
use_cases.description: Generate brand-consistent video content in multiple languages by maintaining character image references while translating the text prompt.
use_cases.target_user: Global brand managers and localization teams

prompt_templates.title: Basic Video Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a 5-second video of [describe your scene, action, and mood]. Medium close-up, natural lighting.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need a simple video without reference materials

prompt_templates.title: Lip-Synced Character Video
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an 8-second video using [describe character from image reference]. [Describe the action and setting]. [Describe the speaker's tone, e.g., warm and friendly]. Medium close-up. 9:16 aspect ratio.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you have a character image and want lip-synced dialogue

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Modal Cinematic Scene
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a 10-second video. Subject from image 1 performs [describe action] in [setting from video 1]. Voice tone matches audio 1. [Additional cinematic direction]. Use the locked shot style from the reference video.
prompt_templates.scenario: When combining multiple reference media types for complex scenes

prompt_templates.title: Brand-Consistent Multi-Language Content
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the character from [brand-character.jpg]. Generate a [duration]-second video in [target language] where the spokesperson says: [translated message]. [Describe tone and style]. Maintain brand lighting and setting from reference.
prompt_templates.scenario: When creating localized content with consistent brand identity

output_examples.input: Generate a 6-second video of a barista explaining today's special in a warm friendly tone. Use the character from character-ref.jpg. Medium close-up, slow push-in, warm cafe lighting.
output_examples.output: A 6-second cinematic video showing a barista (matching the reference image) in warm cafe lighting, naturally lip-synced as they explain today's special offer with a slow camera push-in.

output_examples.input: Create a 10-second 9:16 video where the spokesperson delivers the new product announcement. Reference character from brand-spokesperson.jpg. Calm professional tone, office setting.
output_examples.output: A 10-second vertical video featuring the brand spokesperson (consistent with the reference) delivering the announcement with natural lip-sync in a professional office environment.

best_practices: Put stable identity elements (faces, logos, costumes) in image_url references instead of describing them in text prompts to avoid visual drift
Use plain language for camera direction (e.g., "medium close-up", "slow push-in", "handheld follow") and combine multiple directives for complex shots
Match aspect ratio of reference media to your intended output to avoid unwanted cropping and framing issues
anti_patterns: Describing a character's face in detail in the text prompt instead of using an image reference, which causes identity drift
Mixing radically different aesthetic references (e.g., watercolor style with photorealistic) in the same generation, which confuses the model
Requesting video durations longer than 15 seconds, which will fail with a 422 error - instead, segment into multiple sequential calls

faq.question: What is the difference between Seedance 2.0 Pro and HappyHorse 1.0?
faq.answer: Seedance 2.0 Pro excels at multi-modal reference support and native lip-sync. HappyHorse 1.0 currently ranks higher in blind-vote video quality. Choose Seedance for lip-synced dialogue with character consistency; choose HappyHorse for best overall video quality.

faq.question: How does lip-sync work on Seedance 2.0 Pro?
faq.answer: Seedance generates synchronized speech and lip movement in a single pass when generate_audio is enabled. You can guide the voice tone using descriptive text like 'warm friendly conversational' or 'calm instructional'.

faq.question: Can I use my own audio track with lip-sync?
faq.answer: For audio-driven lip-sync from your own audio file, use Wan 2.7 with the audio_url parameter instead. Seedance generates audio in-pass rather than syncing to provided audio.

faq.question: What aspect ratios does Seedance 2.0 Pro support?
How do I get consistent character identity across multiple videos?
faq.answer: Upload your character reference to image_url. The model maintains identity from the reference image. Avoid describing the character's appearance in the text prompt to prevent drift.

faq.question: What happens if my reference media doesn't match the output format?
faq.answer: The model may crop or reframe your references to match the target aspect ratio. For best results, use reference media with the same aspect ratio as your intended output.
